title: BNCI 2015-007 Motion VEP (mVEP) Speller dataset
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 16 healthy participants performing motion visually evoked potential (mVEP) speller tasks using three different paradigm variants: overt gaze-directed (Overt Cake Speller), covert attention-based (Covert Cake Speller), and foveal motion center stimulation (Motion Center Speller). The study investigates gaze-independent brain-computer interface communication through P300-based visual speller applications with two-level hierarchical selection from 30 symbols. Data were acquired at 100 Hz from 63 electrodes and include preprocessed signals with artifact rejection and baseline correction (epoch window: -0.2 to 1.0 s).
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 16 healthy participants (mean age 23.8 years) using a 63-channel BrainAmp amplifier with actiCap active electrodes in a 10-10 montage. Signals were sampled at 100 Hz with online hardware bandpass filtering (0.016–250 Hz), referenced to linked mastoids with ground at forehead. Offline preprocessing included low-pass filtering (42 Hz passband), baseline correction, and artifact rejection using min-max criterion (≥70 μV) and variance-based methods. Participants performed copy-spelling and free-spelling tasks with visual feedback in a synchronous online mode.
